title: Reverse Nodes In K Group
hints: Start by checking if there are at least k nodes remaining before reversing any group; if not, leave the rest as is.
Use a dummy head node to simplify edge cases and maintain a pointer to the node preceding the current k-group to reconnect it after reversal.
When reversing a group, keep track of the new tail (originally the head of the group) so you can link it to the next group's new head.
